summary: Added routing reason diagnostics tests for label, assignee, and default agent routes. The Router.resolve function already returns routing_reason with source and matched fields, and the orchestrator already logs routing_reason in dispatch logs. Three new tests verify diagnostic coverage for all routing paths.
evidence: mix specs.check: all public functions have @spec or exemption
Router.resolve returns {:ok, %{id: ..., routing_reason: %{source: :label, matched: "agent:mimo"}}} for label routes
Router.resolve returns {:ok, %{id: ..., routing_reason: %{source: :assignee, matched: "user-123"}}} for assignee routes
Router.resolve returns {:ok, %{id: ..., routing_reason: %{source: :default, matched: nil}}} for default routes
Orchestrator dispatch log includes routing_reason=#{routing_reason_log(resolved_agent)} at orchestrator.ex:1483
decisions: Routing reason tracking was already implemented in Router.resolve and orchestrator dispatch logs
Added explicit test coverage for label, assignee, and default route diagnostics as required by acceptance criteria
Existing routing behavior unchanged - routing_reason is additive metadata in the resolved_agent tuple
next_handoff: All acceptance criteria are met. Ready for review.
